history.content:
<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. From the current position, moving forward leads into lava at row 02 column C (`L`), so I should avoid moving forward. I can turn to face down so I can check the cell below (row 03 column B), which is safe (`.`), and continue from there toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B, facing up (^). The goal is at row 08 column G. From this position, forward (up) is a wall (#) at row 01 column B, so I must turn. To progress through the open area below (toward rows 03-08), I should face down (v). Turning right from ^ to v takes two right turns (or one left then right, etc.), so I’ll start by TURN_RIGHT to move toward facing down.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should avoid lava `L`, so first I’ll move forward into the only safe path: from (2,B) to (2,D) is blocked by lava at C, and (2,B) forward goes to C which is lava, so instead I need to turn to face down. That will let me move to row 03 column B, which is floor `.`.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 02 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should avoid lava (`L`) and walls (`#`). Moving forward to row 03 column B is safe (`.`), so I will go forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 03 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should avoid lava (`L`) cells; moving forward to row 04 column B would be safe (it is `.`). So I will move forward one step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 04 column B, facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Forward (down) from here would go to row 05 column B which is floor (`.`), so it is safe and brings me closer vertically toward the goal. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 05 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving toward the goal, the cell directly ahead (row 06 column B) is `.` (safe). So I should move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 06 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Looking ahead, moving forward would go to row 07 column B, which is safe (`.`). So I should move forward to keep progressing downward while avoiding lava (`L`).
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should avoid lava (`L`). From my current position, moving forward to row 08 column B is safe (`.`), so I’ll proceed by moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 08 column B facing down (`v`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward would go into row 09 column B which is `#` (wall), so I can’t. To progress toward the goal, I should head right along row 08 where the cells to the right appear open (C is `L` though, so I must first avoid lava by changing direction). Best immediate safe move is to turn left so I can face right and then choose a safe path without stepping into lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 08 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward from B goes to row 08 column C, but it is lava (`L`), so I must not move forward. The safe option is to turn to avoid lava; turning right from `>` would face down, and the cell below (row 09 column B) is a wall (`#`). Turning left from `>` would face up, and the cell above (row 07 column B) is a floor (`.`). So I will turn left to face up.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 08 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G, but moving right from B would step into column C which is a lava cell (`L`). Also, below B (row 09 column B) is a wall `#`, so I can’t go forward. To make progress safely, I should turn right from `^` to face east (`>`), then move forward only if the cell ahead is safe; currently that will be checked next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column H. Moving forward goes to row 08 column C, which is lava (`L`), so I must not move forward. I can turn to find a safe path; turning right will face down, and the cell below (row 09 column B) is a wall (`#`), unsafe. Turning left will face up, and row 07 column B is floor (`.`), which is safe. So I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 08 column B facing up (^). The goal is at row 08 column G. The cell immediately to the right (row 08 column C) is lava (L), so moving forward is unsafe. I’ll turn right to face east and then look for a safe path around the lava toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 08 column B facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column H. I should move forward to row 08 column C, but that cell is `L` (lava), so I must turn away first. Turning right would face down into row 09 column B which is `#` (wall). Turning left would face up into row 07 column B which is `L` (lava). Therefore, the only safe direction is left into row 08 column A which is `#` (wall) is also blocked—so I need to avoid immediate forward by turning until I can move into a `.` cell. Since forward is lava, I will turn left first to try an alternate route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G. To move toward the goal, I should head right, so I need to rotate from up to right by turning right once.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B and facing right (`>`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward would go into row 08 column C, which is lava (`L`), so it's unsafe. I should turn to change direction; the cell in front of `>` is dangerous, so I’ll turn left to face up and look for a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should move right along row 08, but first the cell to the right (row 08 column C) is lava `L`, so I cannot proceed right. I’ll rotate to face left (`<`) and then move to row 08 column A, which is safe `.` and start working around the lava column.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing left (`<`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward from here would hit lava at row 08 column A, so I should turn right to face up and then navigate toward the open corridor to reach column G.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 08 column B facing up (^). The goal is at row 08 column G. I should move right along the open corridor: the cell to the right (row 08 column C) is L, so I must avoid it. The cell in front while facing up (row 07 column B) is '.' which is safe. I will move forward to row 07 column B to bypass the lava row.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 07 column B facing up (^). The goal is at row 08 column G. From my current position, the cell in front (row 06 column B) is floor '.' and safe, while moving toward the goal requires navigating around lava. First, I will move forward to get closer to the open area above.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 06 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G. From my current spot, the cell in front (row 05 column B) is `.` and safe, so I should move forward to progress downward toward the lower rows.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I’m at row 05 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G. From my current position, moving forward would hit a wall at row 04 column B (it’s `.` but I should avoid getting blocked); however, the more direct progress is to go east along open cells in row 05 (columns C and beyond are blocked by lava at C, but I can first turn to find a safe forward move). Currently the cell in front when facing up (row 04 col B) is `.` so I can move forward safely and keep heading toward the corridor leading east later.
</thought>

<thought>
I’m at row 04 column B facing up (`^`). The goal is at row 08 column G. Moving forward would go into row 03 column B which is `.` (safe), so I will move forward to advance toward the goal.
</thought>

<thought>
I’m at row 03 column B facing up (^). The goal is at row 08 column G. From here, moving forward would go into row 02 column B which is safe (.). To make progress upward toward the open corridors, I’ll move forward.
